quotes.text: One time I went to a petting zoo and a goat tried to eat my shoelaces. I bought the goat, named him Chuck, and he lives in my backyard now. Shoelaces are expensive, Chuck!
quotes.speaker: Shaquille O'Neal
quotes.fake_note: Shaq's playful feud with Charles Barkley and his history of larger-than-life impulse purchases make this very believable. The giveaway is the sheer cartoonish absurdity of buying a goat just to complain to it about footwear costs.

quotes.text: I spent maybe forty hours this week evaluating the spin rate of college long snappers in wet weather conditions. It’s not exactly a hobby, but it keeps my mind sharp.
quotes.speaker: Bill Belichick
quotes.fake_note: Belichick actually did once give a real, passionate 10-minute press conference on the history of long snappers, making this obsession sound incredibly authentic. The tell is the 'forty hours'—even Bill couldn't dedicate a full work week solely to long snapper spin rates.

quotes.text: Just leave me alone, I know what I'm doing!
quotes.speaker: Kimi Raikkonen
quotes.context: Formula 1 driver's deadpan team-radio reply to his Lotus race engineer Simon Rennie, who kept feeding him instructions during the 2012 Abu Dhabi Grand Prix (which Raikkonen went on to win). It became one of the most iconic radio messages in

source.title: 'Leave me alone, I know what I'm doing': the Kimi Raikkonen radio message from a past F1 era - Motor Sport Magazine

quotes.text: I don't watch boxing anymore, I watch bird documentaries. You look at a hummingbird, the way it bobs and weaves to get to the nectar—that’s the exact same head movement Cus D'Amato taught me in '84.
quotes.speaker: Mike Tyson
quotes.fake_note: Tyson has a genuine, well-documented lifelong obsession with birds (specifically pigeons), making this pivot sound highly plausible. However, he never compared the hyperactive flight of a hummingbird to his legendary peek-a-boo boxing style.
